heading: Theft reports
label: 33113 Theft reports
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)3311315:2034., , , § 617; added , , ; , , (e), , 3400.Oct. 20, 1972Oct. 25, 1984Oct. 25, 1992Pub. L. 92–51386 Stat. 947Pub. L. 98–547, § 101(a)98 Stat. 2765Pub. L. 102–519, § 306(a)106 Stat. 3397In this section, the word “information” is substituted for “data” for consistency in the revised title. The word “standards” is substituted for “standard” because there is more than one standard prescribed under this chapter.In subsection (a), before clause (1), the words “” are substituted for “3 years after ” (the date of enactment of the Anti-Car Theft Act of 1992) for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words. In clause (1), the words “distributed for sale in interstate commerce that are” are substituted for “for all such motor vehicles distributed for sale in interstate commerce” for clarity. In clause (5)(A), the word “decrease” is substituted for “have . . . a beneficial impact in decreasing” for consistency and to eliminate unnecessary words.October 25, 1995October 25, 1992In subsection (b), before clause (1), the words “” are substituted for “5 years after ” (the date of enactment of the Anti-Car Theft Act of 1992) for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words. In clause (1)(B), the word “accuracy” is omitted as redundant. In clause (2), the words “distributed for sale in interstate commerce that are” are substituted for “for all such motor vehicles distributed for sale in interstate commerce” for clarity. In clause (9)(A), the word “decrease” is substituted for “have . . . a beneficial impact in decreasing” for consistency and to eliminate unnecessary words.October 25, 1997October 25, 1992In subsection (c)(1)(C), the words “carrying out” are substituted for “the implementation, administration, and enforcement” for consistency and to eliminate unnecessary words.

References in TextThe Motor Vehicle Theft Law Enforcement Act of 1984, referred to in subsecs. (b)(6)(A), (10) and (c)(1)(D), is , , . Titles II and III of that act enacted sections 511, 512, 553, and 2320 [now 2321] of Title 18, Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and , Customs Duties, and amended sections 1961, 2311, and 2313 of Title 18. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 1984 Amendment note set out under , Commerce and Trade, and Tables.Pub. children.heading: .—Truck, Multipurpose Passenger Vehicle, and Motorcycle Report
children.label: a .—Truck, Multipurpose Passenger Vehicle, and Motorcycle Report

children.text: information on the number of trucks, multipurpose passenger vehicles, and motorcycles distributed for sale in interstate commerce that are stolen and recovered annually, compiled by model, make, and line;
information on the extent to which trucks, multipurpose passenger vehicles, and motorcycles stolen annually are dismantled to recover parts or are exported;
a description of the market for the stolen parts;

information on the premiums charged by insurers of comprehensive coverage of trucks, multipurpose passenger vehicles, or motorcycles, including any increase in the premiums charged because any of those motor vehicles is a likely candidate for theft;

children.label: A
children.text: to decrease the theft rate of those motor vehicles;

children.label: B
children.text: to increase the recovery rate of those motor vehicles;

children.label: C
children.text: to decrease the trafficking in stolen parts of those motor vehicles;

children.label: D
children.text: to stem the export and import of those stolen motor vehicles or parts; or

children.label: E
children.text: to have benefits greater than the costs of the identification; and
recommendations on whether, and to what extent, the identification of trucks, multipurpose passenger vehicles, and motorcycles should be required by law.

children.label: A
children.text: the methods and procedures used by public and private entities to collect, compile, and disseminate information on the theft and recovery of motor vehicles, including classes of motor vehicles; and

children.label: B
children.text: the reliability and timeliness of the information and how the information can be improved;
information on the number of motor vehicles distributed for sale in interstate commerce that are stolen and recovered annually, compiled by class, model, make, and line;
information on the extent to which motor vehicles stolen annually are dismantled to recover parts or are exported;
a description of the market for the stolen parts;

children.label: A
children.text: the costs to manufacturers and purchasers of passenger motor vehicles of compliance with the standards prescribed under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: the beneficial impacts of the standards and the monetary value of the impacts; and

children.label: C
children.text: the extent to which the monetary value is greater than the costs;

children.label: A
children.text: making arrests and successfully prosecuting persons for violating a law set forth in title II or III of the Motor Vehicle Theft Law Enforcement Act of 1984;

children.label: B
children.text: preventing or reducing the number and rate of thefts of motor vehicles that are dismantled for parts subject to this chapter; and

children.label: C
children.text: preventing or reducing the availability of used parts that are stolen from motor vehicles subject to this chapter;

information on the premiums charged by insurers of comprehensive coverage of motor vehicles subject to this chapter, including any increase in the premiums charged because a motor vehicle is a likely candidate for theft, and the extent to which the insurers have reduced for the benefit of consumers the premiums, or foregone premium increases, because of this chapter;

information on the adequacy and effectiveness of laws of the United States and the States aimed at preventing the distribution and sale of used parts that have been removed from stolen motor vehicles and the adequacy of systems available to enforcement personnel for tracing parts to determine if they have been stolen from a motor vehicle;

children.label: A
children.text: to decrease the theft rate of those vehicles;

children.label: B
children.text: to increase the recovery rate of those vehicles;

children.label: C
children.text: to decrease the trafficking in stolen parts of those vehicles;

children.label: D
children.text: to stem the export and import of those stolen vehicles, parts, or components; or

children.label: E
children.text: to have benefits greater than the costs of the identification; and

other relevant and reliable information available to the Secretary about the impact, including the beneficial impact, of the laws set forth in titles II and III of the Motor Vehicle Theft Law Enforcement Act of 1984 on law enforcement, consumers, and manufacturers; and

children.label: A
children.text: continuing without change the standards prescribed under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: amending this chapter to cover more or fewer lines of passenger motor vehicles;

children.label: C
children.text: amending this chapter to cover other classes of motor vehicles; or

children.label: D
children.text: ending the standards for all future motor vehicles.

children.label: A
children.text: information reported under this chapter by insurers of motor vehicles and manufacturers of motor vehicles and major replacement parts;

children.label: B
children.text: information provided by the Federal Bureau of Investigation;

children.label: C
children.text: experience obtained in carrying out this chapter;

children.label: D
children.text: experience of the Government under the laws set forth in titles II and III of the Motor Vehicle Theft Law Enforcement Act of 1984; and

children.label: E
children.text: other relevant and reliable information available to the Secretary.
In preparing each report, the Secretary shall consult with the Attorney General and State and local law enforcement officials, as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: cover a period of at least 4 years after the standards required by this chapter are prescribed; and

children.label: B
children.text: reflect any information, as appropriate, from the report under subsection (a) of this section, updated from the date of the report.

At least 90 days before submitting each report to Congress, the Secretary shall publish a proposed report for public review and an opportunity of at least 45 days for written comment. The Secretary shall consider those comments in preparing the report to be submitted and include a summary of the comments with the submitted report.
